topic: Finite Volume Method Basics - From PDE to Algebraic Equations
template_rationale: Theory-heavy topic requiring mathematical derivation of Gauss divergence theorem and its discretization

parts.title: Core Theory - Gauss Divergence Theorem
parts.guideline: Start with control volume concept, derive Gauss theorem from first principles. Show 1D case for intuition, extend to 3D. Include physical interpretation of each term.

parts.title: Physical Challenge - Why FVM Works for CFD
parts.guideline: Explain conservation laws in FVM context. Show owner-neighbor convention with flux signs. Connect to R410A two-phase flow requirements.
key_challenges: Why interpolation is needed (preview of Day 03)

parts.title: Architecture & Implementation in OpenFOAM
parts.guideline: Show Mermaid class diagrams BEFORE code. Explain fvc::surfaceIntegrate line-by-line. Include file paths and line numbers for all verified code.

parts.title: Quality Assurance - Verification Exercises
parts.guideline: Provide 6+ concept check questions with detailed solutions. Include code verification exercises that reference actual source files.

foundation_importance: All FVM discretization in OpenFOAM relies on Gauss theorem
prerequisites: Day 01: Governing Equations - need to know what equations we're discretizing
learning_outcomes: Derive Gauss divergence theorem from integral calculus
Understand control volume concept and mesh topology
Connect continuous PDEs to discrete algebraic equations

connections_to_future_days.connection: Face interpolation - how to get φ_f from φ_P for Gauss theorem
Time derivative combined with spatial divergence
Detailed structure of cells, faces, points
All use Gauss theorem via fvc::surfaceIntegrate

r410a_relevance.fvm_importance: Strict mass and energy conservation required for phase change
r410a_relevance.energy_conservation: Latent heat absorption requires accurate energy flux calculation

progressive_overload_notes.part_1_start: Begin with 1D control volume, add dimensions, derive Gauss theorem
progressive_overload_notes.part_1_progression: Build from integral form to discrete algebraic form
progressive_overload_notes.part_2_shift: Now consider practical CFD challenges - conservation, flux handling
progressive_overload_notes.part_3_shift: Examine how OpenFOAM implements these mathematical concepts
progressive_overload_notes.part_4_shift: Verify understanding through derivations and code tracing

hero_concept.name: Gauss Divergence Theorem
hero_concept.r410a_application: Mass conservation during evaporation requires ∫∇·U dV = ∮U·dS to hold exactly
